1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the formal identification and versioning of the document.  
   Expected Content: Title, version number, status, approval authority, and revision history.  
   Sample Elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Modelling Principles Document  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author  

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected Content: Summary of the modelling principles, objectives, and expected impact on data governance.  
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the foundational modelling principles and scope necessary for effective data governance within the DMD domain.  

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance framework.  
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.  
   Sample Elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Management Domain (DMD)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Overview of the organization’s data governance landscape  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clarify the specific action being addressed by the document.  
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample Elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DMD-01, Define foundational modelling principles and scope  
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish foundational principles for data modelling  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Focus on business concepts and modelling principles  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To detail the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.  
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and a step-by-step execution structure.  
   Sample Elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: Define modelling principles and scope  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Identification of business concepts  
   5.3 Deliverables: Modelling principles document  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Identify Business Concepts  
   - Define Modelling Principles  
   - Scope Definition  
   - Validate Boundary Definitions  
   - Structure the Modelling Principles Document  
   - Review and Approval  
   - Communicate and Implement  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To outline the governance framework supporting the modelling principles.  
   Expected Content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets within the DMD domain  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Model stewardship, terminology, semantic consistency  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Hierarchical representation of governance roles  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Roles and responsibilities of Data Owners and Data Stewards  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Conduct workshops for business concept identification  
   - Draft modelling principles  
   - Review scope with cross-functional teams  
   - Present document for approval  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with other governance domains  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this modelling principles document  

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To define the mechanisms for monitoring and compliance related to the modelling principles.  
   Expected Content: Monitoring, compliance, audit alignment, and escalation pathways.  
   Sample Elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: No measurable KPIs due to Score 0  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: No formal compliance mechanisms due to Score 0  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Describe structural intent only  

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To establish the management and review processes for the document.  
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.  
   Sample Elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by Data Governance Council  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office  

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the document.  
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artefacts.  
   Sample Elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Modelling principles template  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: List of identified business concepts

2. Executive Summary

   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.  
   Sample Elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Management Domain (DMD)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Medium-sized enterprise with evolving data governance practices.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure.  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly articulate the action being defined.  
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample Elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DMD-01 - Define foundational modeling principles and scope  
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish foundational principles for data modeling.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Limited to initial conceptual model development.  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To detail the procedural steps required to execute the action.  
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and step-by-step execution structure.  
   Sample Elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: Draft an initial conceptual model based on defined principles.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Foundational principles must be defined.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Conceptual model v0.1  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Define Modeling Principles  
   - Identify Core Entities  
   - Draft Initial Conceptual Model  
   - Validate Against DAMA Semantics  
   - Assign Stewardship Responsibilities  
   - Align with Lifecycle Checkpoints  
   - Iterate and Refine Model  
   - Finalize and Approve Model  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To outline the governance framework supporting the action.  
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: Data Governance Council oversight.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: Core entities identified in the conceptual model.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data modeling principles, stewardship, lifecycle management.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Office coordination.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Owner responsible for model approval.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Document foundational modeling principles.  
   - Review draft model for compliance.  
   - Assign roles for model stewardship.  
   - Validate model against governance standards.  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Architecture teams.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes operational data management processes.  

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To define the mechanisms for monitoring and compliance.  
   Expected Content: Monitoring, compliance, audit alignment, and escalation pathways.  
   Sample Elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: No measurable KPIs due to Score 0.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: No formal compliance mechanisms established.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Informal pathways for addressing issues.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Not applicable (Score 0).  

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To establish the governance framework for the document itself.  
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.  
   Sample Elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by Data Governance Office.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Owner responsible for document integrity.  

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.  
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample Elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Conceptual model template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous versions of modeling principles documentation.

   Expected Content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All business units involved in data management.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data models within the organization.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, metadata management, and semantic governance.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards, Data Custodians.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for decision-making.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Regular assessment of data models  
   - Feedback collection from stakeholders  
   - Documentation of modelling processes  
   - Continuous improvement reviews  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with other governance domains.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this action.
